text.title: ai-plugin-ecosystem Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-plugin-ecosystem as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Relying on AI plugin outputs (Magician, etc.) for deterministic pipeline results.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI plugin outputs vary with each run; reproducibility is poor and not suitable for deterministic design system workflows.

failure-mode.description: Using Stark plugin as the only accessibility validation step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stark flags contrast issues but cannot audit interaction flow or focus order; it is a partial check only.

failure-mode.description: Running Automator batch operations on the production Figma file without testing on a duplicate first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Batch operations at scale can break component relationships; test on a duplicate file to validate behavior before applying to production.

failure-mode.description: Agents will propose API-driven plugin automation that does not exist — Figma plugin sandbox is browser-JS only, not server-accessible.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Persistent LLM hallucination. No REST API or external trigger surface exists for Figma plugins.

failure-mode.description: LLM output for "write a Figma plugin" is often stale (Plugin API v2 changes); always validate against https://www.figma.com/plugin-docs/.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Figma Plugin API v2 has breaking changes from v1; training data lags reality.

failure-mode.description: Accessibility remediation suggestions from agents (color values, ARIA labels) require a human to apply in Figma — no write API for component props.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents can identify issues but not implement fixes without human-in-loop Figma interaction.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated Content Reel data files may produce culturally biased placeholder names/content; review for i18n/diversity before use.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM-generated names skew toward generic Western conventions; human review required for inclusive design.
